recommendations.title: Migrate data-lake-raw to Glacier
recommendations.reason: Bucket has not been accessed for 90 days and is currently in STANDARD storage class. Data qualifies for Glacier archival storage.
implementationSteps: Create S3 Lifecycle policy for Glacier transition.
Validate compliance requirements.
Transition archived data.
recommendations.executiveExplanation: Data stored in data-lake-raw (15.0 TB) has remained inactive for more than ninety days and should be transitioned to lower-cost Glacier archival storage, saving $9,914.40/year. Estimated carbon reduction: 360.0 kg CO₂/year.

recommendations.title: Move backup-archive to Deep Archive
recommendations.reason: Bucket has not been accessed for 400 days (13 months). Data is extremely cold and suitable for Deep Archive storage.
implementationSteps: Review data retention and compliance requirements.
Create S3 Lifecycle policy for Deep Archive transition.
Validate retrieval time expectations (12+ hours).
Apply lifecycle rule to bucket.
Monitor transition progress in S3 console.
recommendations.executiveExplanation: Data in backup-archive (12.0 TB) has been inactive for 400 days and should be transitioned to Deep Archive storage, achieving 70–90% cost reduction ($9,331.20/year). This also reduces estimated carbon footprint by 432.0 kg CO₂/year.

recommendations.title: Rightsize prod-db (db.r5.xlarge → db.r5.large)
recommendations.reason: Database CPU at 18.0% and memory at 32.0% with 120 average connections indicates underutilization.
implementationSteps: Schedule maintenance window during low-traffic period.
Modify instance class from db.r5.xlarge to db.r5.large.
Monitor database performance metrics for 72 hours.
Verify query performance and connection stability.
Roll back if latency exceeds acceptable thresholds.
recommendations.executiveExplanation: The postgres database prod-db appears significantly underutilized (CPU: 18%, Memory: 32%) and may safely operate on a smaller instance configuration (db.r5.large), reducing annual database costs by $2,102.40.

recommendations.title: Enable Intelligent Tiering for ml-training
recommendations.reason: Bucket access pattern shows 60 days since last access, indicating variable access frequency suitable for Intelligent Tiering auto-optimization.
implementationSteps: Enable S3 Intelligent Tiering on bucket.
Configure archive access tiers if needed.
Monitor cost savings via Cost Explorer.
recommendations.executiveExplanation: Enabling Intelligent Tiering on ml-training (8.5 TB) will automatically optimize storage costs based on access patterns, achieving 10–20% savings ($1,239.24/year). Estimated carbon reduction: 51.0 kg CO₂/year.

recommendations.title: Delete orphaned volume vol-003
recommendations.reason: Volume is unattached and has not been accessed for 90 days. It is classified as orphaned per the 30-day inactivity threshold.
implementationSteps: Confirm backup or snapshot availability.
Notify application owner.
Delete volume via AWS Console or CLI.
recommendations.executiveExplanation: This 200 GB volume has not been attached or accessed for 90 days and represents unnecessary operational spending of $288.00/year. Deleting it eliminates this cost entirely with no performance impact.

recommendations.title: Reduce storage for vol-006 (250 GB → 66 GB)
recommendations.reason: Volume utilization is at 22.0% (55 GB used of 250 GB provisioned). Significant storage capacity is wasted.
implementationSteps: Create a snapshot of the current volume.
Create new 66 GB volume from snapshot.
Attach new volume and verify data integrity.
Delete the oversized original volume.
recommendations.executiveExplanation: This volume is using only 22% of its 250 GB allocation. Rightsizing to 66 GB would save $264.96/year while maintaining a 20% capacity buffer.

forecast.executiveSummary: Cloud spending is projected to reach $22,016 next month, reflecting moderate growth of 7.4%. The three-month forecast averages $21,280/month (range: $22,012–$22,021). Seasonal patterns have been detected in historical spending, suggesting cyclical cost variations that should be factored into budgeting. The overall trend is upward, indicating growing infrastructure needs. Consider proactive optimization to manage cost trajectory.
